parameters.toolDescription: Get a high-level health dashboard across ALL active EDI trading partners. Shows error counts, active/disabled flow counts, network type (VAN/SPS/direct), and EDI document types per partner. Partners sorted by error count (most problematic first). No parameters needed.

include_staging.description: Include staging copies of EDI integrations (default: false)

_meta.description: Wave 0 prototype — stress test: N+2 API calls (1 integrations + 1 flows + N partner error calls), EDI regex parsing in JS, response merging. Validates that n8n can handle the full EDI health dashboard use case.
_meta.note_on_credentials: Create an n8n credential of type 'Header Auth' with Name='Authorization' and Value='Bearer YOUR_CELIGO_API_KEY'. Replace CELIGO_CREDENTIAL_ID in all HTTP nodes.
_meta.note_on_performance: Fetch Errors Per Partner runs once per EDI partner (~25 partners = 25 sequential HTTP calls). This is the latency bottleneck. If too slow, consider adding a gateway endpoint at /api/celigo/edi-health that does this server-side.
_meta.note_on_flow_data: Fetch All Flows fetches all flows in one call. This works because n8n doesn't have the 25MB response size limit that Celigo's PP does. Celigo's GET /v1/flows returns all flows for the account.
_meta.note_on_code_porting: Filter EDI Integrations Code node ports _parse_edi_integration() from extensions/celigo/src/server.py:519-538. Build Dashboard ports _extract_doc_type() from :541-545 and the dashboard aggregation logic.
